We might hope that in the future, after you buy a robot hardware, a Lego toy, and you can insert it directly into our big model, it can do a lot of things.

五十七、Pi的硬件策略

你有一个问题啊,因为你提到你们的硬件有很多的partner。你们自己会做硬件吗?你们做得有多深啊?

为了做咖啡,你是需要另外一个家长。它其实成本都不高,然后换起来也很简单。但是你是有这种研发以后,你才会感觉到什么是一个比较本源的问题,什么是一个其实很简单就可以解决的问题。

I think that the hardware is optimized for software, not for the final task and performance. I think everyone is doing this.

五十八、机器人的GPT时刻还有多远?

This question is so hard to answer. Because I do agree that part of the goal of Py is to become an opening eye for the robotics world, to become a creator of a comprehensive model.

As for where our current big model has developed, I think you see bad things every day. I see that this expression is not very perfect every day. That expression is not good either. How can we make it better? So I think we haven't reached it yet. We haven't reached E. But I think we're getting closer.

你觉得我们什么时候能达到GPT-1?

I think it's because the starting point is different. The starting point of NLP, I just made a short period of time in 2017 when I was gambling. It was funny at that time, you know? I was thinking at that time, I thought it was not good to make it into a natural language.

I switched from natural language to robot. I thought that in the first natural language, you can generate a lot of words. How do you separate these words? How do you know what words you want? It's hard. So I thought it would be hard to do natural language optimization. Because I thought this divider... It's too vague and not that clear.

On the other hand, I thought it was fun to be a robot at the time. Because robots can do a lot of tasks. You can take a picture and think of a new task and then do it. I think this is the fun of being a robot.

五十九、大模型公司的商业化演化

If we go back and look at today's large model development, these model companies are slowly becoming app companies because everyone finds that models are commercialized. It will slowly become unified and unified. So in the end, everyone bought the different and retrofit boxes of the commercial market through a different application to make choices.

I think if we put it in the field of robots, today you may start as a mobile brain, but slowly you may also make a different product form.

你认为机器人会不会有一个独立于大规模模型的独立大脑?

I think it's possible. But at the same time, some people think that all these things can be built into one model. And this model has not been created yet.

六十、Scaling Law与算力

I think scaling law is a very tricky topic. Because there are many things like scaling. There are many different measurement standards in grammar.

For example, scaling is done in the problem. You can run up to the 11th house with the data you collected from 10 houses. Where is your performance? Compared to 100 houses, if you come to this house again, will there be some growth? If you collect 1000 houses again, will there be a big improvement?

你的算力够吗?

I think the computing power is not enough to see if you people like to work. If you people like to work, your computing power will never be enough. You can always have new ideas, new ideas, new ideas, and new models.

六十一、Pi的日常与卷文化

你是一个好的人吗?

I think you can see it from a work market perspective, there are many things. But in fact, it's not that he forced you to stay in the company. It's relatively self-evident.

Today, after having dinner at the company, I started to discuss with this person about a topic. I just want to go down and take a look again. So sometimes people may work for a long time. Even on weekends, a lot of people go to the company. It's relatively self-evident.

你没有加班,对吗?你通常是早上早,晚上晚。作为HIDE研究员的一天是怎样的?

Everyone is different. Some people live in Nanwan and come to the city of Jiujin Mountain. They drive for three hours every day.

But I told the CEO, "Why is it so early at 10 o'clock? Can we change it to the afternoon?" And he was shocked to see me say that they were at 8 o'clock at the earliest, and then they were back at 9 o'clock, and then they were back at 10 o'clock. Why am I still 10 o'clock early?

现在有多少人?

Now, I have about 70 people.

六十二、Pi的创造性文化

你的组织和文化是什么样的?

I think there is no formal way to define it in detail, but we can see that the company has been developing for almost two years. It has some self-evident cultural factors in it.

Overall, I have never worked in other startup companies, but I feel that this is the creative atmosphere I imagined. Everyone is more enthusiastic about doing something that they think is very important.

我们的公司比较特殊,因为它是一个相对开放的研究氛围。So I will organize a reading meeting with another person and some exchanges.

For example, we want to do a project, for example, this Piling Six Star. Piling Six Star has actually been to this library twice. First of all, I want to tell you that we are now exploring this issue. How far have the previous people's publications and things done?

六十三、Cloud Agent带来的生产力革命

Then there are some relatively sky-high lectures, such as Cloud Agent. Then I went crazy and said in the lecture, "Who is the most capable Cloud Agent in our company? Hurry up and let him come on stage and talk about how to use it, and then everyone can copy the homework."

I was busy doing other things and didn't pay much attention to it. Then they introduced us to how they use Cloud Agent. I was shocked after listening to it. I said, "Wow, this is indeed a leap in productivity."

In the last two weeks, I changed my entire workflow. I feel that it's a completely different work idea from a year ago.

六十四、CEO Carl Hausman:效率至上

CEO is Carl Hausman. He's not really focused on the direction of the company's management. Because the research is more about Sergey and Chelsea. So I don't really have much in common with him. He's in charge of some of the company's infrastructure.

六十五、Pi不做人形的原因

I'm worried that their research center will become a better human-centered robot, while my research center will be a better task. I personally believe that a good task without human-centered work is a good task, and a task without human-centered work can be done faster from the perspective of the user.

For example, you see, now we make clothes first. We make clothes, make boxes, make coffee first. You make it first. It doesn't need human-centered work, it can be done.

你不追求所谓的人形机器人,未来世界的统一美?你觉得它可以是任何东西。

我最喜欢的是宠物机器人。I have a friend who said he wanted to start a pet robot business. I was so eager to do it. I would rather join him than do what I'm doing now.

你想成为什么样的宠物?

I want to be a pet. Pet is so cute. I think this is the emotional value of human culture.

六十六、加入Pi的偶然故事

你为什么加入Pai?

It was also a coincidence. Because I actually thought for a long time that I wanted to be a professor. But I was always afraid to be a professor. Because the professor had to write a lot of funding proposals.

Then, because of a very coincidence, Sergey has a student, Abishek. After he graduated, he came to the university I studied at, and became my boss. Because in the past, there was no real business in China that specialized in robotic learning for manipulation.

Because Abyshek was a student of Sergey at that time, and when Sergey was re-creating, Abyshek might have told him, there was a person who was graduating and he was doing this. Sergey said he could talk to him.

I went there and sat down and watched. I don't know if it's an interview. No one told me it was an interview. I was very... It was like, "Oh, there's this person doing this. That's interesting. What are you doing?"

And then they gave me feedback later. They said they felt a great resistance during my interview. Because I kept asking them what they were doing. Oh, you're like this. Is it like this?

六十七、学术界与工业界的抉择

放弃教职选择去派,这与学术界的工作方式和工业界的工作方式有关。

I think there is a bigger problem in the academic world. In fact, you are a student who comes and goes. The academic world in the United States is probably five years. In fact, the United Kingdom is three years shorter.

I thought that if a student came to me and I wanted to give him a chance to do something more open-minded, would he be able to do it in three years?

At the same time, if I do it in the industrial world, there will be more support for both computing, data, and hardware. You can see that the robot is a very diversified one.

六十八、七年博士的价值

说到博士,我突然想到,是不是应该尽快转你的博士?

I think it has a great relationship with personality. I know people who are about to graduate, and I know people like me. We have been in China for nine years. I just entered the school on the first day, and I met someone who stayed in the school for eight years. He graduated in the ninth year.

嗯,所以没有觉得时间过得很慢。你没有很焦虑,就为了毕业很焦虑。没有为毕业很焦虑,也没有觉得就是我没有毕业是个什么样的一个事情。

I was more pure in thinking about what I had done in the past, what I had learned in the past, and what I wanted to do in the next step. It was a very related and happy process.

六十九、Pi vs Google:创业与大厂的差异

你觉得在派工作和在Google工作最大的区别是什么?

I have never worked in Google's research department. I did an internship in software there. I was still a student at the time.

So I believe Google has a kind of engineering gene in it. I really like it. I think it still has a limit. For example, the research they do, the multi-magnetism they do, the TPU they do, all have the engineering optimization idea in it.

And in Py, it's a startup company. I can easily start chatting with the people I want to talk to. I can move fast. Maybe you had an idea yesterday, and today you say it, and tomorrow it becomes a project.

七十、强化学习的意义与探索模块

强化学习对机器人意味着什么?它解决了根本问题吗?

I think strong learning is a very fundamental problem. That is to say, how does a person become better through experience?

In traditional textbooks, the explanation of the "Climbing the Wall" is actually to explain the "dog" of Babelov. The dog did something, you give it a reward. From then on, it knows that this is a good thing and it wants to do more.

一个非常具体的行动。For example, you are a person who wants to learn to swim or to learn to swim. And how do you do better? You just keep doing this thing. I think this pursuit of the ultimate is a very strong factor in strength training, which is to keep practicing.

At the same time, there is actually a very important part of strength training, which is exploration. Exploration, I think it is big and small. It may be that my muscles slightly move to the left, my performance will be better when playing tennis. This is an exploration.

I think in the field of advanced learning, one of the modules is probably the exploration module. If you want to do something better, you have to do something you haven't done before. How do you choose a more likely to succeed thing to explore?

七十一、奖励函数的设计难题

Then the second one I think is a more interesting problem in the field of study, which is the sound of the sound. Maybe you have to ask your pet to do something good. Maybe it did a series of things and you give it a reward. It actually needs to know which thing is a decisive factor for this reward.

You can see that in this big-sword model, you usually use the word "question" to ask a question. In fact, you can fold clothes in all kinds of different ways, right? How do you know what kind of clothes it wants you to fold?

In the textbook, you need to use a reward function to grab something. Maybe this reward function is designed to give you a high reward if you really follow its meaning. It rewards you for doing a good job.

But in terms of practical design, it is very difficult to design this reward function. Because many people have done it before, for example, using a simulation machine to do a learning of the super Mario. You have a score, right? You can just optimize this score.

But in fact, even in this case, it is possible that we say abuse, abuse this reward, it will find that there is a bug. Super Mario wants a bug. After having this bug, the strong-willed snowman will find it and do this bug crazily. He will jump to a small person and then directly pass all the levels and achieve the maximum number of reward functions.

七十二、可验证任务的价值

Tan Jie也提到,情境研究需要奖励信号,但很难写奖励函数。你在这方面发现了什么?

In my opinion, it's not a question of writing a reward function, but a question of what it does to the intelligent body. The reward function is more of a way of expression.

For example, you see NLP now, they should have explored how to write a value function for it. If you do this well, you get a higher score. If you do that bad, you get a lower score. But in fact, there is a period of time in natural language, the most useful thing in natural language learning is the verifiable.

一个任务,这个可验证的任务是可以独立于一个所谓奖励函数存在的,它就是一个——If you can run on the programming, then you are very sure that it is good.

七十三、AFOMap与Chelsea的直觉

这个杯子放在另一个桌子上。但这也是一个非常有限的空间。

For example, a task like folding clothes. Is it good to fold clothes? I don't really fold clothes, so when I'm folding clothes, I have a relatively light-hearted attitude.

你刚才提到Chelsea对一些任务设计有常识。

Yes, I think she has a very sharp intuition.

能举个例子吗?

For example, there are many different strategies for folding clothes, right? Or you can also fold it in four directions. I find it hard to describe how to fold in different ways in a language, but you can imagine that it has different folding methods. So which folding methods, including this folding method, which one is better for the robot? I think some people can really do it very quickly.

And sometimes, when I want you to help me clean up my bookshelf, he can't capture all of my meanings. I might say, "You dropped the trash, and then this bookshelf is closed."

七十四、真机数据的信仰

其他前沿实验室在数据选择上有什么不同?真实数据是共识吗?

I'm not sure if others have consensus or what they think. From the information I've received, it's obvious that some people think we can do better and more useful with the simulation series.

Some people think we should use some "human hands" to collect this kind of "harmful data". People can naturally collect a lot of data in their own space.

But maybe because I'm a believer in real-time data, I think we still need real-time data in the future. Because you don't even have to say it. You can say real-time data. Why not?

你认为什么是好数据?

Yes, I don't think it's a problem that a student can solve clearly. I think there are some different dimensions. For example, what task does this data do?

At the same time, I think there is a quality of action in the task process. This may be if the robot can do this, I don't think it's finished, it's too extreme, it's like an Olympic athlete.

表现就已经超越人体极限了,这个是已经动作质量。你有可能收到一条质量不那么好的数据,可能就是为了拿这个杯子,先晃晃悠悠晃晃悠悠的再去拿,可能就没有那么好。所以这种动作质量也是一个维度。

同时我觉得就是动作的覆盖度吧,就从一个数据集,就刚才可能讲的是具体一条一两条数据,从一个数据集的讲座来说的话,你说了——How much coverage does the task of taking the water bottle have?

七十五、大模型与具体任务的相互促进

同一个模型团队更关注在基础能力层面达到零样本(zero-shot),即在没有看到具体任务的情况下仍然可以工作。而机器人或应用团队往往更关注特定场景、特定身体、特定任务可以运行。这两种需求完全不同。你同意这个观点吗?

I think it's a mutual benefit. These two work centers may not have much to do with each other. But I think mutual benefits are possible.

Because, how to put it, I think robots use two major themes in research. One is how to do it perfectly. Another is how to do something like a model in a new unknown field.

In the process of practice, I often feel that if you can complete a scene, you may achieve better results in more scenes.

你觉得现在哪只脚跑得更快?

I think companies like Pyre are developing very well. Because it is a very original research company. It hopes to solve all the important problems on the way of research.

七十六、加入Pi后的重要变化

你什么时候加入了派?

In 2005. In 2004. Pai was founded in March of 2004. I talked to them in March. I graduated in June and went there.

你觉得过去两年,2024年和2025年,机器人领域最重要的进步是什么?

I actually think that some of the things we do may already have commercial value. This is something that I used to think was a bit far away, but now it feels pretty close.

你能在2024年初做但2024年底做不到的是什么任务?你能在2025年初做但2025年底做不到的是什么?

I think in the beginning of 2024, you told others that you wanted to stack clothes and pile them up. And this clothes is something that you can't control. In the beginning of 2024, I thought that stacking clothes was something I really wanted to do. But I didn't know how to do it.

在2025年,我们做了Py05和Py06。One was to enter Airbnb. The other was to improve the performance of the specific deployment task. It's hard to achieve that with purely data.

七十七、2026年的期待

你对2026年机器人领域有什么期待?

I think there will be more amazing demos this year. But demo itself is one thing, but what is behind it is another thing.

你觉得机器人应该和自动驾驶领域比较,还是和大模型比较?

I think it's more like a big model. I actually think that autonomous driving is harder and simpler than us.

The reason why it's harder for us is that the stake of this kind of driving is very high. If you make a wrong decision, it's a serious problem for human life. So it's extremely high requirement for performance.

And I think they are relatively simple for us. You see, the problem of the control system of the autonomous driving is that there is almost no one. I'm running on this car. They have more problems in terms of decision making. How do you drive below?

Unlike what we often talk about in the task of the control group, I not only have to operate this multi-joint, a more complex machine, and complete a task.

七十八、机器人vs大模型:忍受错误的能力

And compared to the big model, I think the big model is the same as us. The magic of the same work is that the big model can say that it makes mistakes a little bit.

大家都很习惯了嘛,你就是会胡说八道,对吧?我们不会全信你的。所以它的这个完满,我觉得是不需要做得像这么加深那么的严苛。

I think they are... You see, take them as an intelligent body. Their space of action is to output every word. It can also be said to be a strategy. Their space of action is also highly abstracted. So it needs a lot of diverse space of action.

七十九、机器人进入家庭的可能

All robot companies are fighting for a new Beijing standard. Who can be the first robot to enter the family of the delivery? This is a hidden direction that everyone is fighting for.

在家中问题有几个困难的维度。

I think the first one is the complexity of its environment. It's true that there are no two people's homes and bedrooms. It's exactly the same. You have to be flexible.

At the same time, the action complexity of the task in the home is very high. Because people actually take on this housework in the home. It's all kinds of things.

I don't know which company can run a month at home, not only standing there for a month, but also doing various tasks for a month. Sometimes I walk and give the table legs. How should these injuries be dealt with and stabilized?

And if you interact with people in a human environment, there are many dangers. Or maybe this is the scariest thing I've seen in the Tesla Optimus movie. He fell at my house. How much money should I spend to fix my floor? Or if he falls on me, I'm so scared.

变小是更好吗?

I think small is like the height of a child in the tree. It does give me a sense of security. It's not as threatening. And maybe it's a little lighter. After the whole machine is light, it can do things.

八十、中国机器人产业的观察

你怎么看中国机器人在海洋中的发展?

I think I just thought about it a year or two ago and wanted to see this kind of demo. I was very excited to see it really done. I think it can be done, it has a better algorithm and hardware.

I think this hardware might be the most intuitive to China in terms of the industrial comparison between China and the United States. It has leadership and governance. It's hard to imagine that a robot company and assembled a robot, but there is no component in it that is Chinese.

I think China has the advantage of industrial chain leadership. It's too big. And now I don't even know how to chase if Americans want to chase.

八十一、Pi的研究导向:抵制过早商业化

嗯,很偏向研究的一家公司,就是暂时情况下我们应该是完全不考虑生意化,因为觉得那可能是一个destruction分心,这个其实也是有一些历史原因吧,我不知道。

历史原因是什么?

Peter Erbill is the mentor of Sergey Levine. Peter Erbill has had two entrepreneurship experiences. One time, he successfully did a student homework. Now, basically, American universities are using it. I also used this system when I went to college.

At the same time, he also founded a robot entrepreneurship company called Covariant. He founded such a company in 2015 or 2016. He said he wanted to make a robot-only machine learning program. And their company may have deepened logistics and warehouse in the development process.

From the perspective of big model development, I think this is actually a burden for them to develop big models. Because of this investment, although it must have produced a lot of results, but on the big model side, I think it lost its momentum because it went commercial too early.

这种通用繁华性,反而把精力放在了很多这种商业化的相关事情上,没有真的去追溯本源的问题。所以派,我觉得是有受到坠段精力的影响,就是比较的强调说我们不要太想。

八十二、中美硬件与算力对比

But even in the US, companies that are sending out their products——

嗯,我觉得这看你是怎么理解硬件的投入和表现的吧。

We don't have unlimited money. So we can't invest in every aspect. Do you know how much OpenAI has made? We feel so proud every day when we see the money. We also want to have so much investment.

In comparison, the current industry advantage in hardware and media is that in China, there is a very strong supply chain. You can customize a robot, it has a lot of electrical options, robot style options, and they all have a more mature solution.

I think everyone is actually looking at how to make a robot hardware that is most suitable for them. I think everyone is actually doing this.

八十三、软件与硬件的追赶

对中国公司来说,跟随Pi是好的,因为你们很多论文都是公开的。

Yes, I think we have many public papers. Google is not necessarily public, right? Tesla is not necessarily public, but they can follow you. Even if Google is public, if you want to catch up with Google, you have to first create a large model like Gemini. This is already a bar.

I think the same logic applies to companies like Pi. So the company is constantly accumulating. Even if it tells you what it does, you don't necessarily have so many things to go to.

八十四、公司内部对中国机器人的关注

你们公司的其他人会关注中国机器人的发展吗?

I think they are all very concerned. This is an indispensable part.

For example, the performance of "Rainy Days" was posted in the company's group chat. Everyone discussed how to do it. Even recently, I want to invite some experts to talk about it. Because we don't do human-like things, we don't do local motion. We can also ask them to teach us how to develop these things.

Shi Guanya is a professor at CMU. He became friends with her when he was in the US. He learned a lot of local motion from her. Their team has recently released Omni Target.

Now it can be used to achieve the attitude you want with the power of learning. Power learning is very important. Power learning has achieved considerable success in the local motion world.

八十五、美国硬件投入的困境

你觉得美国对硬件的投入够吗?足够重视吗?

I think many of the engineers in the US will agree that our hardware has a lot of room for improvement.

特斯拉是怎么做到的?

Tesla is very mysterious. We actually don't know the technical details of its hardware. It won't tell us these things.

而且好像是有一个笑话,我不知道是不是真的,只是一个笑话,说特斯拉最牛逼的工厂是在上海开的,产量就是高。相对而言确实它是已经有一部分是造业的吧,我觉得这个科目——

It's also related to the American education system. You see, the professionalism and employment in education, the lack of this industry in the past has also led to the lack of a batch of talents. It's not easy to make up for it.

八十六、机器人意识的哲学思考

你觉得人工智能最后会有意识吗?机器人会有意识吗?

What a lucky fate! You know, every time I see a robot, it's always awakening. It wants to destroy humanity.

I want to say I really hope someone can explain to me how consciousness was born. Because we are just some biologists, but we will listen to some of these science fictions. I really want to figure out how it was born from some physics, chemistry, and these things. I don't even know how it represents.

I think if we think that the consciousness generated by robots is like human consciousness, I'm a little pessimistic. I want to say that it's really possible. But if you define consciousness very broadly, for example, I'm talking to AI now. If the feeling it gives me is that it is like a person, that's enough. Then you can't say it has no consciousness.

八十七、机器人的种族与自我繁衍

这将是什么样的机器人比赛?

I think the robot race is interesting. What I've always wanted to do is that robots can be themselves. I think this is a process that robots have developed.

Because it's like a race, it's continuous. It can be developed by itself. I think robots can be assembled and be themselves. It's a reflection of the development.

So this is also a topic that I really want to do. When I was looking for a teacher, I said I really wanted to do this.

I think the robot's race is very good. I like the robot that can be fixed wherever it is broken. You see, after this rock fell, I had to stay at home for a long time because of a certain part of the injury. And the robot can be replaced. Then you can remove it and change the shape. I think this is a very free way of existence.

八十八、机器人版的忒修斯之船

Just like the Greek story of the ship of Tertius, I changed the robot from head to toe into another part. Even the model can be changed. It's still the robot.

In fact, in the development process of our company, there is this kind of, originally there was a number, this is a robot, its number is this. Then through continuous repair and modification, it is no longer the original one. It seems to be the only connection with that original one, that is, its number is the same, but it has been changed entirely.

We said, should I give it a new name? Otherwise, it's so complicated, I often say that this robot is such a thing at a certain time, and it is such a thing later.

八十九、机器人是否是人类最后的创造?

你认为机器人比赛是人类比赛的延续吗?机器人是人类做的最后一件事吗?

The last thing? I think so. I hope that many things we do can improve productivity, so that you can do a lot of things that people need to do, and then you can do it alone.

But I believe that human creativity is infinite. I don't think we live to improve productivity. I think we live more for love and death, the eternal chess in literary works.

But a more romantic idea is that although our lives are very short, only 100 years, put in human history, put in the history of the universe, it is a small and unimportant issue. But is it possible to create something that can see the end of the universe?

九十、黑盒与懒惰的人性

这种训练机器人的方法,与上一代相比是非常不同的。它是一个黑盒。你对人类和机器人有什么新的感受吗?

它是黑盒,但是它因为是从一个数据出来的东西。它是有限制的,它是bonded by the objective that you define. 它是一个很数学化的一个东西。所以它虽然是一个过于一致的,对,它是一个过程无法理解的黑盒,但是它是在数学角面上可以解释的一个东西。

我觉得在训练机器人的过程中,有什么感悟的话,可能一方面会觉得,确实就是能够发挥我这个特别想偷懒的天性吧。如果一件事情可以让机器人来带我做,I will think of ways to make this promotion come to me.

I think this is my sloth for the human civilization. I can even discuss whether sloth is a fundamental pursuit of the human civilization. Increasing productivity and sloth are the same thing.

九十一、机器人实现后要做什么?

当机器人实现时,你会做什么?

Realize it? Write a novel. Go home and write a novel. Make a game. Create a story that fits my taste very well.

你现在脑海里有这样的故事吗?

I think that the story of ghost novels has never stopped. It's another thing whether it's written or not.

Yes, I think we don't need to be criticized by our parents who visit us and say that our house is so messy and dirty. We can leave things we don't want to do to others and leave them to other robots.

Maybe there are two repairmen who can do anything. They can cooperate with each other. They can do things that need to be done by different species.

九十二、喜爱的小说世界观

你喜欢的小说的世界观是什么样的呀?

有两类吧,第一类是在一个完全不同的科幻环境下探索的一些人与人之间的关系。The second type is a magical subject, but put it into our modern life.

For example, the first type, I have seen a real-life novel, but it is about technology development in the universe. It's very abstract, and I think it's a mess. But it's actually exploring a——What kind of social system? What kind of social system does it transform into?

In the social system, there are many gender contrast elements. The division of men and women is completely different. The division of women is because they have their own space in the universe. So they are shaped into a kind of world that looks like a woman's world.

九十三、悲观主义与生活的意义

你是悲观主义者吗?

I think so.

你觉得悲观主义怎么样?

我觉得我很难在看到一个具体的意义的陈述的时候认同它。或者是为了什么,我觉得是一个无解的命题。

So when others try to convince me that something is meaningful, I will be able to get a conclusion if I want to break the sand. It's actually meaningless.

生命的意义是什么?

It's meaningless. I don't think I need to answer this question.

是什么激励你每天生活?

I think I'm very happy in a way. Because I'm doing what I want to do.

I like what Qiao Buci said before. There are many points in life. When you walk through them, they may not be relevant, but maybe one day they will connect. I don't think every point I walk through needs a clear goal to drive me.

九十四、推荐一本人生之书

我们会请每位嘉宾推荐一本对你的人生产生了非常重要影响的人生之书。

觉得有非常多的书都影响到了我,但是现在可能偶尔会思考的这本是Distinction吧。constructional social taste。

让我对于我所喜好的一些东西,和我有多少喜欢是我自己喜欢,有多少喜欢是社会的塑造的喜欢,产生了更多的认知。而在现在这个社会在硅谷,我觉得是从事着一种对于生产力的崇拜的。

Maybe we don't have the old-fashioned way of playing the ball and riding the horse as described in the book. It's a more noble movement. In those days, they might pursue something that we don't agree with.

Although I am so enthusiastic about optimizing productivity at work, although the entire GuiValley has given up these concerns about clothes and clothing, it is concerned about efficiency, it is concerned about ingenuity, it is concerned about creation. It is also not a part of this social value.

九十五、在硅谷:反抗单一价值观

当你年轻的时候,你想离开你的家乡。你想知道外面的世界是什么。来到外面的世界。然后在这一刻,你在硅谷。因为今天的硅谷实际上是统一的。这是高度竞争的。你有什么要抗拒的吗?

I often think this is a very interesting point. I got into the competition when I was a child. I will learn some algorithms.

贪心算法和动态规划是两个经常放在一起的东西。The tactic is that I have to make the most optimized decision at every time point. I want to do the best at the moment. And dynamic planning will tell you that if you have some perspective of the whole situation, you will find that every step is the best decision and does not allow you to achieve the best solution of the whole situation.

I really like this logical information. So sometimes, I think it's like a horse in the field, or a horse in the field. A moment of success or failure, and optimization, and whether you can do it or not, doesn't mean the end result of the long term.

And my own learning process and things like that may also be seen by many people as never doing things to the end. I think maybe it's the first time for me to think that we have to do this thing to the end. But so many things that I didn't do before made me walk this path today.

九十六、爱丁堡:一个偏爱的城市

全球范围内你最喜欢的一种食物。

I used to like to stay alone. And now the big cities are often the same. I don't feel like I'm in a completely different physical space. My whole state changes so much.

There are a lot of ancient buildings in Edinburgh. It's well preserved. And it doesn't have much of a big city feel. It's like a tourist city. Everyone goes to see the castle.

But in that city, there is a feeling of selling. There is a kind of thing that is so heavy in history that you put in front of you and then you want to make a very big dynamic change in a moment of turning. But in front of these heavy history, where will this dynamic change go?

九十七、一个鲜为人知但很重要的知识点

一个鲜为人知但很重要的知识点。

嗯,可以。它会在第一次的时候会破裂,会流血,就变成了一个大家非常相信的一个真理了。但其实在医学方面,它是有一个医学的名称叫做阴道斑。

九十八、影响机器人进程的重要因素

在你看来,是什么影响了机器人的进程?

I think from the perspective of tree development, it has affected many things, including the electronics of this generation, such as diffusion policy, and the use of transformer structure like Transformer ACT. I think there are many original studies of imitation and evolution studies that have affected what people are doing now.

在你目前的理解中,一个关键和重要的事实是什么?

I think we don't necessarily enter the home in the form of products, but in the form of more explorations. Not that far away.

你的意思是机器人在家里?

Yes, it's closer than I thought.

九十九、语言即世界:关于工作室的名字

我们的工作室叫做"语言世界工作室"。你第一次听到这个名字时想什么?

I was subconsciously thinking that language is the whole world. Then I thought that this is the brain of the steel. We live in a place where all senses are connected. All your senses control your perception of the world.

You can imagine that we are living in a giant simulator. Your eyes, nose, ears are all plugged into a signal source. This signal source can give you whatever you want.

Because now in the research period, everyone will say that the large-range model is a pure language interaction. In the previous generation, the pure language interaction, it can't understand the real world. Can't understand a concept and put it in the real world to prove that it is a water cup.

This water cup actually has a lot of physical properties that cannot be captured, cannot be captured, cannot be described in words. I think it is also such a reflection that has led to the development of multi-modal research.

那我们是否生活在一个巨大的模拟器中?

If the simulator is the world, then it seems that there is no difference between modeling and not modeling. It's a bit of a superstition.

结尾

Alright, that's all for today's program. This is Business Interviews, a deep-dive interview program produced by the Language and World Studio. You can go to the official account to follow our studio and get more information. Our official account is Language and World, Language is World. You are also welcome to have more interactions with us in the comments section of Little Universe. We hope to explore a new world with you from here.

观看原视频 →
item.description:
## 一、令人意外的观察：写小说和做机器人是同一件事的两面
柯丽一鸣同时是研究员和网文作者，这看似是"理性与感性"的分裂人格，但深挖下去会发现一个反直觉的真相——**她做的是同一件事：都是在探索"生产力剧变后，人与自我的关系如何重构"**。
她说自己是"懒惰主义者"，把强化学习当作让机器人自己练习、把自己从劳动中解放的工具；同时她写科幻小说，探索的核心命题也是"生产力爆炸后人做什么"。**这不是巧合——她其实在用两种介质做同一个田野调查**：白天在硅谷的实验室里，她用代码"实验"未来；晚上在网文里，她用叙事"预演"未来。研究是她的forward pass（前向计算），小说是她的backward pass（反向传播）——两者共同构成一个闭环：现实中做出的东西反哺想象，想象中推演的社会形态又倒逼她思考自己在做什么。
这解释了一个更深的悖论：为什么她说自己是"悲观主义者"，却每天热情地推进robot brain？因为她根本不需要"意义"来驱动自己——她的驱动来自贪婪算法和动态规划的辩证：**每一步做局部最优不等于全局最优，所以九分就够，剩下一分留给意外**。这种"结构性懒惰"，恰恰是这

item.title: [Dwarkesh Clips] Russia Is the Mongol Empire Running in Reverse – Sarah Paine
item.dc:creator: Dwarkesh Clips
item.content:encoded:
💡 创造性洞见

一、蒙古帝国的时间倒放：一个被忽视的镜像

Paine 说俄罗斯是"倒放的蒙古帝国"——这句话表面是地理描述，实际上暗藏着一个更深的熵学式观察。蒙古人从草原向外辐射，以移动性征服定居文明；俄罗斯人则从定居的莫斯科向外辐射，以行政化吞并游牧空间。两者路径相反，但都在解决同一个问题：如何让一个没有天然边界的中心稳定存在。

真正令人意外的是：蒙古人失败于"消化不良"——他们征服了太多东西却来不及行政化，帝国迅速分裂；而俄罗斯人发明了双层行政制度（guberniya + oblast），本质上是给征服设了一个"胃"。地图上你看到的那些红线圈出的 oblast，不是行政单位，而是帝国的消化酶。这是人类历史上极少数把"消化过程本身"制度化的政治发明。

二、边界会移动的国家 vs 边界不动的国家：一个跨领域连接

Paine 顺口提了一句极其锋利的对比：美国的州界一百多年没变，而俄罗斯和中国的省界一直在动。大多数人把这当成"制度成熟度"的差异，但换个角度看，这其实是两种截然不同的"信息压缩策略"——

  • 美国式：冻结边界，让人口、资本、文化在固定容器里流动。边界是常量，内容是变量。
  • 俄中式：流动边界，用行政切割来重组族群、稀释抵抗、创造依赖。边界是变量，人口是被动的常量。

这让我想到生物学里的两种免疫策略：脊椎动物的适应性免疫（记住每一个入侵者，形成固定抗体库）vs 无脊椎动物的先天免疫（不断重组识别模式，靠灵活性应对未知）。美国是适应性免疫型国家，俄罗斯是先天免疫型国家。这不是文明高下，而是面对威胁密度的不同工程解——因为俄罗斯面对的是 Paine 说的那个惊人事实：一次要应对 6 个方向的邻居。

三、"内线优势"的隐藏账单：为什么俄罗斯注定偏执

Mackinder 说内线通信是俄罗斯的优势——铁路让它可以快速把兵力从一端调到另一端。Paine 却轻描淡写地补了一刀：内线优势的对价是"多邻居诅咒"。360 度都是威胁。

这里藏着一个反常识的洞察：俄罗斯的偏执不是文化问题，是拓扑学问题。英国是岛国，威胁是一维的（海上来的）；美国有两大洋，威胁基本是零维的（几乎没有）；而俄罗斯的威胁是二维环形分布——任何一个方向的松懈都可能致命。

这解释了一个很多西方分析师至今困惑的现象：为什么俄罗斯在冷战结束、看似"安全"之后依然扩张？因为在环形威胁拓扑里，"缓冲区"不是选择，是数学必需。你无法在一个 360° 都是邻居的地方玩"防御性收缩"——每退一步，暴露的周长反而更长（想想圆的周长公式：半径缩小，暴露面减少得比你想象的慢）。这跟分形海岸线悖论是同一种数学：越靠近本土，边界的复杂度反而越高，防守成本反而越重。

所以 Paine 这段看似讲历史地理，实际上是在告诉你一件事：理解俄罗斯不需要读普京的心，读一张环形拓扑图就够了。这是把心理学问题降维成几何问题的漂亮示范。

📝 转录文本

一、Mackinder 的关注点：为什么是俄罗斯，而不是中国

Now, Mackinder didn't much care about China one way or the other, because in his lifetime China was a country in free fall. So he's focusing on Russia, the continental empire.

Mackinder's really concerned about Russia also because it's building railways that are going to give it all these internal lines of communication. And Mackinder thinks that's going to work really well for them in wartime — whereas Britain is stuck with exterior lines of communication. Exterior, because you're going by sea and on the periphery of things.

二、俄罗斯：一个"倒放的蒙古帝国"

So, if you look at the Russians, they're like Mongols in reverse. They start in Moscow, and then Moscow lays waste to the other cities — princely city-states. And after that, they're after furs, making money that way. And the Russians keep going until they run into countries that fight back.

三、1914 年的俄罗斯帝国地图：行政单位的秘密

So, to show you how this works — this is the Russian empire at its height in 1914. You can see Moscow, the historic capital. And you can look at the colors there and see: "Ooh, the administrative units are fairly small around Moscow." And they get really big as you go out into Siberia. And these are civil administrative units.

And here's a clearer map — except it's not really clear. The bigger these units are, they're provinces; they're called guberniya in Russian — governorates. But anyway, these provinces are standard administrative units.

But for the recently ingested and yet-to-be-digested, they're put in under different units called oblast, oblasty. And you can't see them on a map — they're in great print. So this shows you more or less where they're located. Outlined in red is where these places are. These are the places in process of being digested.

四、民政边界之上，还有军事边界

So these are civil borders. These are the military borders that are superimposed over this — but only on the conquered areas do you have governor-generalships. The "general" should give you a hint of what's really going on.

And who's conquered? Finland, Poland, Ukraine, Caucasus, all the khanates of Turkestan, Siberia.

And China has also done this — of having provincial boundaries that come and go and move around, and also superimposing military regions that also move around. Whereas if you look at, like, the United States, boundaries of states, they really haven't changed — it's you got one set of states, and then that's that.

But what's going on here is: these boundaries are a mechanism of ingesting, digesting, and if you can't fully digest and turn everybody into a regular province, at least you can neutralize these ethnic groups.

五、内线通信的代价：多邻居的诅咒

All right. So Mackinder's all about the advantages of internal lines of communication. Well, it comes with a corresponding disadvantage, which is multiple neighbors.

And these are Russia's no-kidding security threats circa 1900. And from west to east there: Britain, Germany, Austria-Hungary, the Ottoman Empire, China, and Japan.

This security world is really different from Britain's 360° "you can't get me" moat. Lots of people have come to get Russians over the years. And Russians have fought constantly with Germans, the Austro-Hungarian Empire, the Ottoman Empire. They have suffered catastrophic defeats to the Mongols, the Swedes, initially to Napoleon, the Germans in World War I, and, uh, in the Cold War — but each time the central position allowed them to…

If you enjoyed this clip, you can watch the full episode here and subscribe for more clips. Thanks.

观看原视频 →

item.title: [Zhang Xiaojun Podcast] Kay Ke: Inside Physical Intelligence's Open-Source Robot Models and the Robotics Landscape
item.dc:creator: Zhang Xiaojun Podcast
item.content:encoded:
💡 创造性洞见

一个意料之外的"元观察"：转录与标题的错位本身就是信息

你提供的转录内容并非 Kay Ke 谈 Physical Intelligence 开源机器人模型的内容，而是一段 Google Chrome 的开场白（"Hey everyone, Mark here from Google..."）。这看似是一次数据事故，但它恰好揭示了当下 AI + 视频内容生态的一个隐藏结构性问题：标题层、转录层、嵌入广告层在流水线中是彼此解耦的。YouTube 的 pre-roll 广告、赞助口播和创作者主体内容会在 ASR（自动语音识别）管道里被无差别地拼在同一条 transcript 上，若上游只截取了前 N 秒，就会出现"标题指向机器人，文本却指向浏览器"的鬼影。这对任何做 RAG / 内容摘要的人都是一个具体警告：转录数据的"污染带"通常集中在前 15 秒与末尾 30 秒。

跨领域连接：Chrome 的开场白与 Physical Intelligence 的机器人模型，其实在讲同一件事

表面上一个是浏览器、一个是机器人基础模型，但它们共享一个更深的结构：都是"通用底座 + 开发者生态"的双层架构。Chrome 之所以强大，不在于渲染引擎本身，而在于它把"网页"抽象成一个可被数十亿开发者复用的执行环境；Physical Intelligence 走的 π0 / π0.5 路线也是同一个赌注——把"物理动作"抽象成一个可被无数机器人形态复用的策略底座。换句话说：Chrome 是数字世界的 VLA（Vision-Language-Action）之前身——它把"看到网页 → 理解意图 → 触发动作（点击、表单、支付）"这条链固化成了一个开发者可编排的运行时。机器人基础模型今天正在做的，是把这套"开放底座 + 开发者杠杆"的剧本，从像素世界搬到关节扭矩世界。

打破常规的判断：真正的瓶颈不在模型，而在"内容管道的可信度"

大多数关于机器人 / AI 的讨论都聚焦在参数、数据、算力。但你今天遇到的这个具体场景——"我拿到的转录不是我以为的转录"——其实预示了 2026 年最被低估的一个瓶颈：AI 时代的信任危机不会先发生在模型输出层，而会先发生在"输入语料的溯源层"。当播客、访谈、开源模型的技术细节都要通过多层自动管道（ASR → 清洗 → embedding → LLM 摘要）被下游消费时，任何一层的错位都会被后续模型放大成"看起来合理但完全错误"的洞察。这是一个反直觉的判断：未来最值钱的不是更大的模型，而是"可验证的、带出处哈希的语料层"——某种意义上，这正是 IPFS、C2PA、乃至区块链在等待的那个杀手级用例。你今天遇到的这个小小的转录错位，是那个大问题的显微镜切片。

📝 转录文本

开场：来自 Google 的问候

Hey everyone, Mark here from Google.

主题引入：开放网络与 Chrome 的演进

Today we're talking about the power of the open web and how Chrome is evolving.

致敬开发者生态

Developers are the heart of this ecosystem, building amazing experiences for billions of people.

观看原视频 →
